_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.shape: The yield curve graphs Treasury yields across maturities from 1 month to 30 years. A normal curve slopes upward, indicating longer maturities carry higher yields. An inverted curve slopes downward, often preceding recessions by 6 to 18 months. Currently, the 10-year Treasury yield is 4.55%, and the 10-year minus 2-year (2s10s) spread stands at 0.39 points, showing the curve has re-steepened above zero after a period of inversion throughout 2022-2024.
notes.signal: The 2s10s spread is a key recession indicator, as an inverted curve (negative spread) has historically predicted recessions. The recent spread of 0.39 points marks a recovery from the deepest inversion of 0.0 points in the data series. Despite the re-steepening, it is noteworthy that yield curve un-inversions often occur shortly before recessions onset. Meanwhile, the NY Fed recession probability remains elevated at 31%, reflecting ongoing recession concerns amid the yield curve dynamics.
notes.now: The current upward slope and positive 2s10s spread suggest a reduction in recession risk compared to recent years of inversion. However, the still elevated 31% recession probability and the historical pattern of late un-inversion imply caution. The curve presents a mixed signal, indicating risk has moderated but economic uncertainty persists.
